> I've got no clue on issue on first log, can @LF team explain?

in your jjb, the verify job is checking it is able to build a docker
it is triggers on any file on master (file: '') so also for this INFO.yaml
you may change the trigger to consider only the Dockerfile change

- project:
    name: 'oom-readiness-docker'
    jobs:
      - gerrit-docker-verify
      - gerrit-docker-merge
    project-name: 'oom-readiness'
    project: 'oom/readiness'
    stream:
      - 'master':
            branch: 'master'
            files: ''
    docker-name: 'onap/oom/readiness'
    docker-build-args: '-f ./Dockerfile'
    docker-root: '$WORKSPACE'
    mvn-settings: 'oom-settings'
    build-node: ubuntu1804-docker-8c-8g

but in your repo you do not have a Dockerfile..it is funny there are logs, but no error logs except 
Build step 'Execute shell' marked build as failure, which is a bit light..

Patch Set 3:

@lfit teams, is the INFO.yaml correct?

Patch Set 3:

Yes, the INFO file looks good and it was verified against the schema successfully. 
The issue is reported by the docker verify job in https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-readiness-docker-verify-master/3/ which looks to me like a cred issue.. let me take a peek

Patch Set 3:

Found the issue, the "oom-readiness-docker-verify-master" job was using "oom-settings" which did not had a docker.io credential which I have added now and the job should work ok. 
However, I would recommend this job to be updated to use "oom-readiness-settings" instead for consistency.

Or, we can add the initial code to make that job pass and then rebase the INFO.yaml..
